Sales Invoices

Sales invoices are outbound invoices you issue to your clients. Merlin numbers them automatically in the format SINV-YEAR-NNNNN (for example, SINV-2026-00001). You can create them manually, or let recurring invoice schedules generate them for you. Once created, you can send them by email directly from the application.

Overview

Go to Billing › Sales Invoices to see all sales invoices. The list shows the invoice number, client, issue date, due date, total, and status. Status reflects the invoice's progress: draft (not yet sent), sent (emailed to the client), partially_paid, paid, and voided (cancelled; terminal).

Creating a sales invoice

  1. Go to Billing › Sales Invoices.
  2. Click New Invoice.
  3. Select a client. The receivable account and payment term pre-fill from the client's defaults.
  4. Set the issue date and (if required) the due date.
  5. Add line items.
  6. Save.

The invoice starts in draft status. You can edit it freely until it is sent.

Invoice fields and line items

NEEDS CONFIRMATION: exact validation rules on the sales-invoice form fields.

Each line item has a description, quantity, unit price (excluding tax), and tax rate. Line totals are calculated as quantity × unit price. The tax amount for each line is calculated from the line total and the tax rate.

Tax and totals

Merlin calculates five summary figures for each sales invoice:

FieldMeaning
SubtotalSum of all line totals (excluding tax)
Tax totalSum of all tax amounts across all lines
TotalSubtotal + tax total
Amount paidTotal payments recorded against this invoice
Balance dueTotal − amount paid

The tax label (e.g. VAT or GST) and default tax rate are configured in Settings › Purchasing.

Sending an invoice

Click Send Invoice on the invoice detail panel to email the invoice to the client. Merlin renders the invoice using the Unlayer email template selected in Settings › Billing and delivers it via NettMail. The invoice status changes to sent after a successful send. Requires the can-send-sales-invoices permission. See Email (NettMail) for how to set up and customise email templates.

Recording payments

When a client pays, click Record Payment on the invoice. Enter the payment amount, date, an optional reference (such as a bank transaction ID), and the bank account the payment was received into. The invoice's amount_paid and balance_due are updated immediately. If balance_due reaches zero, the invoice status changes to paid automatically.

Recording payments requires the can-record-payments permission. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: distinction between can-record-payments and can-process-invoice-payments.

Voiding an invoice

Voiding cancels an invoice permanently. The invoice status is set to voided, which is a terminal state — a voided invoice cannot be re-activated or edited. Use void when an invoice was issued in error or needs to be replaced by a corrected invoice. Requires the can-void-sales-invoices permission.

PDF generation

Merlin generates a PDF of each sales invoice using the BillingService and the barryvdh/laravel-dompdf package. The generated PDF can be downloaded from the invoice detail panel. The PDF uses the company details from Settings › General (name, address, tax number, and logo).

Permissions

  • documents-view-any / documents-view / documents-create / documents-update — to read and write sales invoices
  • document-lines-create / document-lines-update / document-lines-delete — to manage line items
  • can-send-sales-invoices — to email an invoice to the client
  • can-record-payments — to record a payment against an invoice
  • can-void-sales-invoices — to void an invoice
